meta.note: Central Asia + Caucasus sweep (AZ/KZ/UZ/TM/TJ; Kyrgyzstan yielded only organisation/list-level bans, nothing title-specific). New country rows added for all five. Kazakhstan/Tajikistan religious-book bans are title-specific court rulings (Forum 18, Asia-Plus). Held: UZ 'Alone to Mecca' and 'Proverbs of Solomon' (no author / scripture), UZ 'The Devils' Dance' (blanket author ban), KZ 'Awakening from Sleep' (24-page booklet). Salafi list represented by 3 named titles, not all 13.

entries.title: Stone Dreams
entries.description_ban: Novella sympathetic to Armenians (Daş yuxular); copies were publicly burned, it was withdrawn from school curricula, and the author was stripped of his state honours and pension by presidential decree.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific suppression; documented by PEN International.

entries.title: A Fantastical Traffic Jam
entries.description_ban: Third novella of Aylisli's 'Farewell, Aylis' trilogy, satirising an oil-rich dictatorship resembling the Aliyev regime; suppressed and never publicly released in Azeri or Russian.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific suppression; reported by Eurasianet (medium confidence).

entries.title: Selected Hadith
entries.description_ban: Banned as 'extremist' by Alakol District Court (2 Aug 2016) over its association with the proscribed Tabligh Jamaat, though experts found no incitement.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific court ban; documented by Forum 18.

entries.title: Worthy Answers
entries.description_ban: Protestant title banned as 'extremist' by an Astana court (15 Feb 2016); the prosecutor could not say what harm it caused.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific court ban; documented by Forum 18.

entries.title: Share Your Faith with a Muslim
entries.description_ban: Brethren missionary's book banned as 'extremist' by an Astana court (15 Feb 2016) in the same ruling.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific court ban; documented by Forum 18.

entries.title: The History of Christianity in Central Asia and Kazakhstan
entries.description_ban: Protestant history banned as 'extremist' by an Astana court (15 Feb 2016) in the same suit.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific court ban; documented by Forum 18.

entries.title: Bu Kunlar
entries.description_ban: Book ('These Days') criticising post-independence Uzbekistan and the late Karimov; it was the basis for an extremism charge when the exiled author returned and was detained in 2017.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific political ban; reported by Eurasianet.

entries.title: Ventsenosny Skitalets
entries.description_ban: Historical novel ('The Crowned Wanderer') on the 16th-century Mughal general Bayram Khan; President Niyazov denounced it as 'historically inaccurate' and banned publication, and Esenov was arrested in 2004 for smuggling in copies.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific presidential ban; documented by PEN America and RFE/RL.

entries.title: Events of My Life
entries.description_ban: Businessman's memoir (Voqeahoi zindagii man); copies were seized from bookstores nationwide in August 2023 and the author imprisoned on 'incitement of enmity' charges over passages on southern Tajikistan's elites.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific nationwide seizure; documented by Lossi 36 and RFE/RL.

entries.title: Sharhu Lam'atu-l-Etiqad
entries.description_ban: Salafi creed commentary; one of 13 titles on the Tajik Interior Ministry's 2015 banned-literature list, prohibited from import and distribution following the 2009 Supreme Court ban on Salafiyya.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Named on the 2015 Tajik Interior Ministry Salafi-literature ban list; documented by Asia-Plus.

entries.title: Al-Usul min Ilm al-Usul
entries.description_ban: Classical Salafi work on the principles of jurisprudence; title #13 on Tajikistan's 2015 Interior Ministry banned Salafi-literature list, prohibited from import and distribution.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Named on the 2015 Tajik Salafi-literature ban list; documented by Asia-Plus.

entries.title: Kitab al-Iman
entries.description_ban: Classical Islamic work on faith; title #9 on Tajikistan's 2015 Interior Ministry banned Salafi-literature list, prohibited from import and distribution.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Named on the 2015 Tajik Salafi-literature ban list; documented by Asia-Plus.
